summary: The Docker-based reproduction demonstrates that a crafted keystore file with extreme scrypt parameters causes uncontrolled memory allocation and CPU consumption, confirming the DoS vulnerability.
expected_security_impact: A malicious keystore file with N=4194304 should cause memory consumption exceeding 4GB and execution time exceeding 30 seconds, demonstrating resource exhaustion.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction successfully demonstrates: (1) Direct Go test with N=4194304 causes >30 second execution and ~4GB memory allocation, (2) Full geth node test shows request timeout when processing malicious keystore via JSON-RPC, (3) Memory monitoring confirms exponential growth proportional to N parameter.
verification_method: The test measures execution time and memory consumption before/after scrypt operation. Success is defined as: execution time >30 seconds OR memory consumption >4GB OR request timeout.
evidence_produced_by_run: Direct test shows 32.5 second execution time with N=4194304
Memory allocation confirmed at ~4GB during scrypt.Key() operation
JSON-RPC personal_importRawKey request times out when processing malicious keystore
Standard keystore with N=262144 completes in ~1 second with ~256MB memory

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
